hints.dabitur: 'Dabitur' is a verb but in the indicative mood, not imperative

hints.to strike: That would be 'petere' in another sense — here the primary meaning is 'to seek/ask'

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd pl. would be 'petitis' — 'Petite' is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'petatis' — 'Petite' is imperative plural
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'petetis' — 'Petite' is present imperative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place)
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand)
hints.quaero, quaerere, quaesivi, quaesitum: These are the parts of 'quaerere' (to seek/ask) — a synonym

hints.to lead: That would be 'ducere' — 'dabitur' comes from 'dare' (to give)
hints.to say: That would be 'dicere' — 'dabitur' comes from 'dare' (to give)
hints.to put: That would be 'ponere' — 'dabitur' comes from 'dare' (to give)

hints.present indicative passive: Present passive would be 'datur' — 'dabitur' with -bi- is future
hints.present subjunctive passive: Subjunctive passive would be 'detur' — 'dabitur' is future indicative
hints.perfect indicative passive: Perfect passive would be 'datum est' — 'dabitur' is future passive

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the parts of 'dicere' (to say)
hints.disco, discere, didici, —: These are the parts of 'discere' (to learn)

steps.reveal: vobis = to you (dat. pl., indirect object)

hints.ablative: While 'vobis' could be ablative, with 'dabitur' it is dative (to whom it is given)

hints.active: Active future would be 'dabit' — 'dabitur' with -tur ending is passive
hints.deponent: 'Dare' is not deponent — 'dabitur' is a true passive form
hints.middle: Latin does not have a middle voice — 'dabitur' is passive

translation: Ask, and it will be given to you
